interfaces.name: SpendEntry
interfaces.signature: @Model final class SpendEntry { var id: UUID; var amount: Decimal; var category: Category?; var loggedDate: Date; var weekOfYear: Int; var weekYear: Int; var note: String?; var receiptPhotoRelativePath: String?; var createdAt: Date }
interfaces.notes: No @Attribute(.unique). weekOfYear/weekYear snapshotted at insert via WeekCalculator, never recomputed live. category relationship optional with inverse for CloudKit compatibility.

interfaces.name: Category
interfaces.signature: @Model final class Category { var id: UUID; var name: String; var glyphName: String; var sortOrder: Int; var isCustom: Bool; var createdAt: Date; @Relationship(inverse: \SpendEntry.category) var entries: [SpendEntry]? }
interfaces.notes: Free tier ships ~9 fixed non-custom categories seeded on first launch.

interfaces.name: WeeklyTarget
interfaces.signature: @Model final class WeeklyTarget { var id: UUID; var category: Category?; var amountLimit: Decimal; var isActive: Bool }
interfaces.notes: Visual-only, no notification scheduling anywhere in the type or its consumers.

interfaces.name: SpendRepository
interfaces.signature: protocol SpendRepository { func fetchEntries(weekStart: Date) async throws -> [SpendEntry]; func fetchEntries(categoryID: UUID) async throws -> [SpendEntry]; func addEntry(amount: Decimal, categoryID: UUID, date: Date, receiptPhotoPath: String?) async throws -> SpendEntry; func updateEntry(id: UUID, amount: Decimal, categoryID: UUID) async throws; func deleteEntry(id: UUID) async throws; func fetchCategories() async throws -> [Category]; func addCategory(name: String, glyphName: String) async throws -> Category; func recapData(weekStart: Date) async throws -> RecapCardData; func importCSV(data: Data) async throws -> CSVImportResult; func exportCSV() async throws -> Data }
interfaces.notes: The whole future-CloudKit/bank-import promise depends on every View/App-Intent call site going through this protocol, never ModelContext directly.

interfaces.name: SwiftDataSpendRepository
interfaces.signature: final class SwiftDataSpendRepository: SpendRepository { init(modelContainer: ModelContainer) }
interfaces.notes: Only concrete implementation in v1. CSV dedupe is an application-level Set<UUID> check against fetched entries, never a DB uniqueness constraint.

interfaces.name: RecapCardData
interfaces.signature: struct RecapCardData: Equatable, Codable { let weekStart: Date; let weekEnd: Date; let totalAmount: Decimal; let entryCount: Int; let categoryBreakdown: [CategoryTotal]; let weatherReading: WeatherReading; let generatedAt: Date }
interfaces.notes: Plain value type only, zero @Query/@Environment dependency, so it can feed both the on-screen Recap view and the ImageRenderer export identically.

interfaces.name: CategoryTotal
interfaces.signature: struct CategoryTotal: Equatable, Codable { let categoryID: UUID; let categoryName: String; let glyphName: String; let amount: Decimal }
interfaces.notes: Element type inside RecapCardData.categoryBreakdown.

interfaces.name: WeatherReading
interfaces.signature: enum WeatherReading: Equatable, Codable { case notEnoughData; case insufficientHistory(totalAmount: Decimal, entryCount: Int); case sunny(totalAmount: Decimal); case cloudy(totalAmount: Decimal); case stormy(totalAmount: Decimal) }
interfaces.notes: notEnoughData when entryCount < 3 regardless of amount. insufficientHistory when entryCount >= 3 but fewer than 2 comparable prior weeks exist to baseline against — shows total only, no relative claim.

interfaces.name: WidgetState
interfaces.signature: enum WidgetState { case uninitialized; case empty(weekStart: Date); case data(RecapCardData) }
interfaces.notes: TimelineProvider must switch exhaustively over this; .uninitialized only reachable via a thrown/caught store-open error, never a fallback from try?.

interfaces.name: WeekCalculator
interfaces.signature: enum WeekCalculator { static func weekBounds(containing date: Date, calendar: Calendar) -> (start: Date, end: Date); static func weekOfYear(for date: Date, calendar: Calendar) -> (week: Int, year: Int) }
interfaces.notes: Must respect calendar.firstWeekday (locale-aware), not hardcode Sunday/Monday. Pure function, unit-tested across locales.

interfaces.name: WeatherAlgorithm
interfaces.signature: enum WeatherAlgorithm { static func reading(for currentWeek: (totalAmount: Decimal, entryCount: Int), priorWeeks: [RecapCardData]) -> WeatherReading }
interfaces.notes: Baseline is trailing average of prior logged weeks, not a fixed dollar threshold. Degrades to .insufficientHistory below 2 comparable prior weeks.

interfaces.name: CSVService
interfaces.signature: enum CSVService { static func parse(data: Data) -> Result<CSVParseResult, CSVImportError>; static func serialize(entries: [SpendEntry], categories: [Category]) -> Data }
interfaces.notes: Hand-rolled parser, no third-party dependency. Export includes a stable UUID column used for import dedupe.

interfaces.name: CSVParseResult
interfaces.signature: struct CSVParseResult { let newEntries: [ParsedCSVEntry]; let duplicateCount: Int; let totalRowCount: Int }
interfaces.notes: Feeds the CSV Import Preview screen's counts UI.

interfaces.name: ParsedCSVEntry
interfaces.signature: struct ParsedCSVEntry: Identifiable { let id: UUID; let amount: Decimal; let categoryName: String; let date: Date }
interfaces.notes: Row-level parsed record shown in the import preview table before commit.

interfaces.name: CSVImportError
interfaces.signature: enum CSVImportError: Error, LocalizedError { case emptyFile; case missingRequiredColumn(String); case malformedRow(line: Int, reason: String); case unreadableEncoding }
interfaces.notes: Every case must produce a specific human-readable errorDescription, never a generic 'import failed'.

interfaces.name: EntitlementStore
interfaces.signature: @Observable final class EntitlementStore { private(set) var isPlusActive: Bool; func refresh() async; func purchase(_ product: Product) async throws; func restore() async throws }
interfaces.notes: Defaults isPlusActive = false on any unknown/unreachable entitlement state; never blocks UI with a spinner.

interfaces.name: AppRoute
interfaces.signature: enum AppRoute: Identifiable { case logSheet(prefill: LogPrefill?); case entryDetail(UUID); case categoryDetail(UUID); case paywall(trigger: PaywallTrigger); case csvImportPreview(CSVParseResult) }
interfaces.notes: Single router owns this; driven identically by in-app taps, widget deep link URL, and App Intent completion.

interfaces.name: LogPrefill
interfaces.signature: struct LogPrefill { let amount: Decimal?; let categoryID: UUID? }
interfaces.notes: Used when a deep link arrives with partial data pre-filled.

interfaces.name: PaywallTrigger
interfaces.signature: enum PaywallTrigger { case newCategoryLimit; case widgetConfig; case customTag; case trendCharts }
interfaces.notes: Determines paywall copy context; every trigger must resolve to a dismissible sheet with a working Not Now.

interfaces.name: RecapCardView
interfaces.signature: struct RecapCardView: View { let data: RecapCardData; init(data: RecapCardData) }
interfaces.notes: Plain value-driven View, zero @Query/@Environment access, safe to feed directly into ImageRenderer for both on-screen display and ShareLink export.

interfaces.name: DayCard
interfaces.signature: struct DayCard: View { enum State { case logged(entries: [SpendEntry]); case noEntry }; let date: Date; let state: State }
interfaces.notes: Single component with a state enum, not two views, so the logged-vs-no-entry honesty distinction can't drift out of sync.

interfaces.name: MoneyText
interfaces.signature: struct MoneyText: View { enum Size { case display, large, small }; let amount: Decimal; let size: Size }
interfaces.notes: Only sanctioned way to render a dollar amount; always applies serif token, never used for non-money numbers.

interfaces.name: LogSpendIntent
interfaces.signature: struct LogSpendIntent: AppIntent { static var title: LocalizedStringResource; @Parameter var amount: Double; @Parameter var category: CategoryEntity; func perform() async throws -> some IntentResult }
interfaces.notes: Calls SpendRepository.addEntry directly; proves the write path independent of Siri-facing phrasing polish.

interfaces.name: CategoryEntity
interfaces.signature: struct CategoryEntity: AppEntity { var id: UUID; var name: String; static var typeDisplayRepresentation: TypeDisplayRepresentation; static var defaultQuery: CategoryEntityQuery }
interfaces.notes: AppEntity wrapper resolving Category rows for Shortcuts/Siri parameter selection.

interfaces.name: SpendWidgetProvider
interfaces.signature: struct SpendWidgetProvider: TimelineProvider { func placeholder(in: Context) -> WidgetState; func getSnapshot(in: Context, completion: @escaping (WidgetState) -> Void); func getTimeline(in: Context, completion: @escaping (Timeline<WidgetState>) -> Void) }
interfaces.notes: Store-open wrapped in explicit do/catch; must never let try? collapse .uninitialized into .empty.

interfaces.name: CSVImportResult
interfaces.signature: struct CSVImportResult { let importedCount: Int; let skippedDuplicateCount: Int }
interfaces.notes: Returned by SpendRepository.importCSV after commit; feeds a post-import confirmation summary.
